external_field_name: # Q-020 — External-catalogue field comparison (first-pass; single analyst; 2026-05-28).
# Per-class summary lives in the sibling .md.
# Substrate model under audit: ArgumentScheme + CriticalQuestion + SchemeInstance + ArgumentSchemeInstance + SchemeVariant.

semantic_description: Argdown

external_source: # re-derivable from existing fields (substrate_mapping = derivation recipe)

external_field_name: #   externally-load-bearing-internally-irrelevant    # not needed under substrate ontology (rationale required)

semantic_description: Type of rule of inference for the rule-application (RA) node
rationale: Direct mapping — substrate scheme key plays the RA-Scheme-Type role

semantic_description: Type of conflict for the conflict-application (CA) node
rationale: Derivable: ruleType=strict CQs with attackKind=rebut → CA-Rebut; attackKind=undercut → CA-Undercut

semantic_description: Type of preference for preference-application (PA) node
rationale: Preferences are first-class in the substrate via the Preference table and ASPIC+ preferenceLevel; not a scheme-layer concern

semantic_description: Type of transition for transition-application (TA) node — dialogue-protocol-level
rationale: Dialogue protocol lives in the Dialogue/Locution layer not on schemes; substrate's ludics/dialogue layer carries this

semantic_description: Free-text descriptor of the scheme
Logical-form description (typically first-order or modal)
rationale: Re-derivable from aspicMapping ruleType + premises + conclusion JSON; no canonical formal-logic string is stored

semantic_description: Citation/provenance of the scheme (e.g.
rationale: accidental-omission
follow_on_q: Substrate has no per-scheme provenance field. Should be added for catalogue-import audit trail and to disambiguate `expert_opinion` vs `expert-opinion` cases

semantic_description: Information-node type (proposition vs statement)
rationale: I-nodes are argument-level not scheme-level; substrate models them on Claim/ClaimText

semantic_description: Locution-node illocutionary force (assert query etc.)
rationale: Locutions live in the dialogue layer not on schemes

semantic_description: Temporal validity of the scheme (when applicable)
rationale: AIF spec allows period annotations on schemes for historical/legal corpora; substrate's epistemicMode field handles factual/hypothetical/counterfactual but not temporal validity. Not load-bearing for the substrate's argumentation use case

semantic_description: Which version of the AIF specification this scheme conforms to
rationale: Substrate's AIF round-trip (lib/aif/syncArgument.ts) does not record version. Spec 4 phase 4c AIF round-trip identity discipline needs version-pinning

semantic_description: Canonical URL for the scheme record on AIFdb
rationale: Substrate is the canonical source for its own schemes; external URLs belong on import-provenance not on schemes

semantic_description: Identifier of the AIFdb argument-map this scheme was extracted from
rationale: Should live on an ImportProvenance side-table not on ArgumentScheme. Spec 4 phase 4c benefits from this when round-tripping

semantic_description: User who added the scheme to AIFdb
rationale: User attribution lives on substrate's own audit log (createdById on Argument) not on the abstract scheme

semantic_description: Version history of scheme edits
rationale: Substrate scheme edits are tracked via Postgres audit triggers and Prisma migrations not in the scheme row itself

semantic_description: Mapping into AIFdb's JSON nodeset format
rationale: Spec 4 phase 4c serialiser builds this from premises + conclusion + CQs + aspicMapping at round-trip time

semantic_description: When the scheme was imported
rationale: Substrate has no per-scheme `importedAt` or `createdAt`. The Q-019 audit script discovered this (ArgumentScheme has no createdAt column). Should be added for catalogue-hygiene

semantic_description: Stable identifier for the scheme
Free-form tag list
Markdown heading depth in the source Argdown document
rationale: Argdown headings are presentation-layer; substrate's hierarchy lives in parentSchemeId / clusterTag

semantic_description: YAML metadata block at the top of an Argdown file
rationale: Substrate's per-scheme metadata is structured (separate columns); a free-form frontmatter dict is anti-pattern under the substrate ontology

semantic_description: Argdown's arrow syntax for relations (<_, _>, <+, +>) between claims/arguments
rationale: Substrate has typed claim/argument relations (support/attack/undercut) covering the Argdown arrow set

semantic_description: Manually-set XY coordinates for graph layout
rationale: Layout is a UI-layer concern (recomputed per render); substrate stores no layout state on schemes

semantic_description: Cross-references between argument cells (`[Statement1]`)
rationale: Substrate models cross-claim references via ClaimEdge / ArgumentEdge at the argument layer not the scheme layer

semantic_description: Boolean operators on relations (`AND`/`OR` between premises)
rationale: Substrate models premises as independent items each with its own CQs; conjunction is implicit and disjunction is rare in Walton-style schemes

semantic_description: Identifier for the defeasible/strict rule
strict | defeasible
Numeric preference among rules
Defines what contradicts what (¬a vs a)
rationale: Contrariness is a global system-level function in ASPIC+; substrate models contradictions via per-claim ClaimRelation rebut/contradict edges not via a per-scheme contrariness operator

semantic_description: Kn (necessary) | Kp (ordinary premises) | Ka (assumptions) | Ki (issues) classification of premises
rationale: Substrate uses Carneades's premiseType which maps onto ASPIC+'s Kn/Kp/Ka taxonomy at round-trip time; see Spec 3 phase 3d

semantic_description: Preference principle for argument-tree evaluation
rationale: System-level evaluation policy; substrate's evaluation uses Carneades-style proof-standard not ASPIC+ link policies

semantic_description: Distinguishes attacks on conclusion vs on inference
rationale: attackKind ∈ {rebut undercut undermine} mirrors ASPIC+ distinctions

semantic_description: Whether a premise is axiomatic (cannot be attacked) or ordinary
rationale: Substrate's premiseType taxonomy covers the substantive distinction; an `isAxiomatic` boolean would make it explicit at the CQ level

semantic_description: Pattern for when this rule is defeated
rationale: Substrate models defeaters as CQs with attackKind; round-trip serialiser builds the pattern

semantic_description: Rule whose attack creates a dialectical issue
rationale: Substrate models issues via dialogue-layer Open/Close locutions; round-trip would build from CQ status transitions

semantic_description: Whether this scheme requires its warrant to be made explicit (Toulmin-style)
rationale: Substrate's premise templates always make the warrant explicit (it's the major premise) so the marker is redundant

semantic_description: Justification structure linking conclusion to grounds via warrant
rationale: Re-derivable from substrate's structural fields; no canonical justification string is stored

semantic_description: Scheme name
Named premise templates
Conclusion template
Named CQ list with attack semantics
Position in WRM 2008's hierarchical scheme tree (Ch. 9)
rationale: Substrate's hierarchy fields cover this but tree-position metadata (depth root-distance siblings) is computed not stored

semantic_description: Source-document chapter/section for this scheme
rationale: Substrate has no per-scheme citation pointer back to WRM 2008. Required for honest catalogue provenance

semantic_description: Direct subsumption relation
Concrete worked examples for each scheme
When this scheme is applicable
Predicate type of the conclusion (proposition typology)
Subject type of the conclusion
rationale: Substrate has no per-scheme subject-typology field; Wagemans's PTA requires this for full taxonomy placement

semantic_description: Type of relation between premise and conclusion
Predicate of object vs predicate of statement
rationale: Derivable from conclusionType + reasoningType combination but not explicit

semantic_description: is vs ought axis
